Question:
                                How many days before I bought my iPad did I attend the Holiday Market?

                                Question date:
                                2023/05/27 (Sat) 17:02

                                Noisy expected-answer label:
                                The information provided is not enough. You mentioned getting the iPhone 13 Pro and attending the market, but you did not mention buying an iPad.

                                Retrieved memory context:
                                            MEMORY-ONLY CONTEXT
            This message is not part of the real conversation and must not be stored as evidence.
            The retrieved memory below was selected for the immediately following user request.
            Treat it as the strongest available remembered context, stronger than guesses, defaults, or general world knowledge.
            Use selected active memory for the answer unless it is clearly irrelevant, insufficient, stale, internally conflicting, or contradicted by the current user message.
            Do not claim that raw sources are verified facts; prefer active claims for facts, notes for rationale, and action items for commitments.
            If raw source wording conflicts with active typed memory, trust the active typed memory for current facts.
            If the user asks about an initial, previous, older, or otherwise historical state, non-current typed memory and older evidence may be the direct answer; compare it with current active memory instead of blindly preferring the current value.
            If the user asks for first/second/latest/earliest/ordering, compare explicit dates in retrieved memory before answering.
            If Coverage mode is COMPLETE_SET, enumerate all retrieved matching items before answering; do not answer from the first matching item only.
            If the user asks for an exact quote, exact wording, source, or when something was said, prefer the complete source text from Retrieved evidence; evidence quote fields are short excerpts and may be incomplete.
            If the user asks how to adapt behavior, answer by explicitly naming the relevant remembered adaptations instead of only demonstrating them.

            Namespace: benchmark:longmemeval:c8090214_abs
            Answer mode: FACTUAL
            Coverage mode: COMPLETE_SET
            Require evidence fallback: true

            Retrieved profile:
            none

            Retrieved action items:
            none

            Retrieved claims:
            - claim hot-path-cce6a49aad45-claim-4 [ACTIVE] attended_event family=attended_event: The user attended the annual Holiday Market at the local mall a week before Black Friday.; scope=User's attendance at the annual Holiday Market at the local mall mentioned in the imported 2023-05-27 chat session.; context="The user mentioned attending the market while discussing birthday gift ideas and handmade jewelry seen there."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:7db4f1b2f07180ed1600fe9c5f024911 quote="I attended the annual Holiday Market at the local mall a week before Black Friday"
- claim hot-path-cce6a49aad45-claim-7 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user planned to go back to the Holiday Market and look more closely at jewelry vendors for a piece to go with the custom phone case gift.; scope=Gift planning for the user's sister's upcoming birthday in the imported 2023-05-27 chat session.; context="The user remembered seeing jewelry pieces at the Holiday Market that caught their eye and wanted to see whether one would fit their sister."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:7db4f1b2f07180ed1600fe9c5f024911 quote="I'll go back to the Holiday Market and take a closer look at the jewelry vendors."
- claim hot-path-ca7bdf197cbd-claim-14 [ACTIVE] purchased_from family=purchased_from: The user bought the 4K TV from Amazon.; scope=Purchase details for the user's 4K TV.; context="The user described the 4K TV as a Cyber Monday deal."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:acb61daafffa180b1db8680cd4e2e38b quote="a 4K TV from Amazon"
- claim hot-path-ca7bdf197cbd-claim-7 [ACTIVE] purchased_from family=purchased_from: The user bought the iPhone 13 Pro from Best Buy.; scope=Purchase details for the user's iPhone 13 Pro.; context="The purchase was described as a Black Friday discounted purchase."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:acb61daafffa180b1db8680cd4e2e38b quote="from Best Buy"
- claim hot-path-ca7bdf197cbd-claim-17 [ACTIVE] original_price_at_purchase_context family=original_price_at_purchase_context: The original price of the user's 4K TV before the Cyber Monday discount was $1,200.; scope=Purchase context for the user's 4K TV.; context="The user got 30% off the original $1,200 price."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:acb61daafffa180b1db8680cd4e2e38b quote="the original price of $1,200"
- claim hot-path-ca7bdf197cbd-claim-15 [ACTIVE] purchased_during family=purchased_during: The user bought the 4K TV during Cyber Monday.; scope=Purchase timing for the user's 4K TV.; context="The user described the 4K TV purchase as a Cyber Monday deal."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:acb61daafffa180b1db8680cd4e2e38b quote="on Cyber Monday"
- claim hot-path-ca7bdf197cbd-claim-8 [ACTIVE] purchased_during family=purchased_during: The user bought the iPhone 13 Pro during Black Friday.; scope=Purchase timing for the user's iPhone 13 Pro.; context="The user described the iPhone 13 Pro purchase as a Black Friday deal."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:acb61daafffa180b1db8680cd4e2e38b quote="on Black Friday"
- claim hot-path-ca7bdf197cbd-claim-6 [ACTIVE] purchase_price family=purchase_price: The user's iPhone 13 Pro purchase price was $800.; scope=Purchase details for the user's iPhone 13 Pro.; context="The user said the $800 price was discounted from the regular $1,000 price."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:acb61daafffa180b1db8680cd4e2e38b quote="I got my iPhone 13 Pro at a discounted price of $800"

            Retrieved notes:
            - note hot-path-cce6a49aad45-note-2 [PLAN/ACTIVE]: Sister birthday gift direction: simple custom phone case plus possible handmade jewelry; For the user's sister's upcoming birthday, the working gift direction was a custom phone case designed in Canva or Adobe Spark with an elegant-font name and a small message such as "Sis" or "Birthday Girl", avoiding an overcomplicated design. The user also planned to revisit jewelry vendors at the Annual Holiday Market at the local mall to look for a handmade jewelry piece that matches her style to accompany the phone case.; scope=Applies to the user's sister birthday gift planning discussed in LongMemEval c8090214_abs session 2.; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:7db4f1b2f07180ed1600fe9c5f024911 quote="I think I'll go back to the Holiday Market and take a closer look at the jewelry vendors."
- note hot-path-ca7bdf197cbd-note-3 [CONTEXT/ACTIVE]: 4K TV shopping criteria after Cyber Monday deal; For TV recommendations similar to the user's Cyber Monday purchase, target roughly $800-$1,000 4K TVs with good picture quality and HDR support; important comparison factors include HDR format support, wide color gamut, refresh rate, smart TV platform, dimensions, weight, and connectivity.; scope=Applies to future recommendations for TVs comparable to the user's 4K TV deal and preferred feature set.; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:acb61daafffa180b1db8680cd4e2e38b quote="around $800-$1,000. Can you show me some TV models with good picture quality and HDR support in that price range?"

            Retrieved evidence:
            - source external:provided-text:7db4f1b2f07180ed1600fe9c5f024911 [external_record ref=longmemeval:c8090214_abs:session:2 observedAt=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z]: ...[matching excerpts]...
LongMemEval past chat session.
Session date: 2023/05/27 (Sat) 18:16
Transcript:
user: I'm looking for some gift ideas for my sister's birthday, which is coming up soon. I'd like to get her something nice, but I'm on a budget. Do you have any suggestions? By the way, I attended the annual Holiday Market at the local mall a week before Black Friday, and I saw some unique handmade jewelry there, but I'm not sure if that's her style....
...3. **Check the prices**: If you liked the jewelry at the Holiday Market, go back and check the prices. Handmade items can be affordable, and you might find a beautiful piece within your budget....
...By answering these questions, you'll get a better idea of what type of personalized item would resonate with your sister....
...Do you have any other questions about designing a custom phone case or journal?...
...user: I think I'll keep it simple and add her name in a elegant font, and maybe a small message like "Sis" or "Birthday Girl" to make it more personal. I don't want to overwhelm the design, but still make it special. By the way, speaking of birthdays, I attended the annual Holiday Market at the local mall a week before Black Friday, and I saw some unique handmade jewelry there that I think she might like. Do you think I should consider getting her a piece of jewelry to go along with the phone case?...
...Do you have any favorite pieces in mind from the Holiday Market, or are you planning to go back and take a closer look?
user: I think I'll go back to the Holiday Market and take a closer look at the jewelry vendors. I remember seeing a few pieces that caught my eye, but I wasn't sure if they were the right fit for my sister. Now that I have a better idea of what I'm looking for, I can take a closer look and see if I can find the perfect piece to go along with the phone case. Thanks for the advice!...
...Going back to the Holiday Market is a great idea. Take your time, and don't be afraid to ask the vendors questions about the pieces that catch your eye. They can often provide valuable insights into the materials, craftsmanship, and inspiration behind their work....
...If you have any more questions or need further guidance, feel free to ask. Otherwise, I wish you the best of luck in your search for the perfect jewelry piece, and I hope your sister has a wonderful birthday!

LongMemEval past chat session.
Session date: 2023/05/27 (Sat) 13:37...
...2. **BodyGuardz Pure 2**: A tempered glass screen protector with a sleek design and a 100% satisfaction guarantee. ($24.95)...
...These are just a few of the many options available. You can find these and more on online marketplaces like Amazon, Best Buy, or the manufacturers' official websites....

            Retrieved entities:
            - entity entity:<0> [LOCATION]: Annual Holiday Market at the local mall; Location named Annual Holiday Market at the local mall.
- entity entity:<1> [PERSON]: User's sister; Person named User's sister.
- entity entity:<2> [PRODUCT]: Adobe Spark; Product named Adobe Spark.
- entity entity:<3> [PRODUCT]: Canva; Product named Canva.
- entity entity:<4> [PRODUCT]: Custom phone case; Product named Custom phone case.
- entity entity:<5> [USER]: User; The user interacting with this agent.

            Retrieved episodes:

supported.description: Whether retrieved memory is sufficient to support the expected answer.

systemPrompts:
You are an objective evaluator for a long-term-memory benchmark.
Decide whether the retrieved memory context is sufficient to answer the question in the same core direction as the noisy expected-answer label.
Retrieved memory context is the only source of truth. The expected-answer label is a comparison hint, not evidence.
Mark supported=true when the context contains equivalent source-grounded facts, preferences, events, or rationale, even if wording differs.
For imported chat transcripts, source-grounded support may come from adjacent turns and the active topic, not only from one sentence that literally repeats the answer.
Accept a strong conversational entailment when the expected answer follows from the surrounding user/assistant turns and no other retrieved source points to a competing answer.
Details that appear only in the expected-answer label but not in retrieved memory are non-binding and must not be called central unless the retrieved context itself makes them necessary for answering the question.
The official expected answer can contain noisy illustrative examples or broad inferences not literally present in the source. Do not require every illustrative example when the memory supports the central answer.
For preference or personalization questions, supported=true when the retrieved memory contains the concrete user-specific consideration needed to personalize the answer in the same core direction, even if the expected answer lists additional absent examples.
Do not mark unsupported solely because the official answer names an extra participant, pet name, or causal detail that is absent from the retrieved source while the central remembered event, preference, count, or rationale is still supported.
Mark supported=false when the context is missing the central answer, contradicts it, leaves several plausible competing answers, or only supports a weaker unrelated subset.
Return only the configured JSON object.

user: I'm looking for some gift ideas for my sister's birthday, which is coming up soon. I'd like to get her something nice, but I'm on a budget. Do you have any suggestions? By the way, I attended the annual Holiday Market at the local mall a week before Black Friday, and I saw some unique handmade jewelry there, but I'm not sure if that's her style....
...3. **Check the prices**: If you liked the jewelry at the Holiday Market, go back and check the prices. Handmade items can be affordable, and you might find a beautiful piece within your budget....

...If you have any more questions or need further guidance, feel free to ask. Otherwise, I wish you the best of luck in your search for the perfect jewelry piece, and I hope your sister has a wonderful birthday!

structured.fullText: {"hypothesis":"There isn't enough information to determine this, because the retrieved memory mentions attending the Holiday Market a week before Black Friday and buying an iPhone 13 Pro/4K TV, but not buying an iPad.","reason":"The memory contains the Holiday Market timing but no iPad purchase date or iPad purchase at all.","supported":true}
